_comment: Library of fully-generated supporting characters referenced from story prompts (imagegen/prompts/<type>_<n>.json) as {TOKEN} placeholders. Maintained by the `character-config` skill. The input-photo person (the protagonist) is NEVER defined here -- only the conjured supporting cast. See README.md for the placeholder/runtime contract.

runtime.resolution: Strip the curly braces from a placeholder found in a prompt string, look up characters[<TOKEN>].description, and substitute it in. This is the same flat string-replace mechanism workflow.py already uses for USER_ID / STORY_ID. characters[*].description is the ONLY field read at runtime; everything else in this file is authoring metadata.
runtime.fallback: If a token has no entry under `characters`, compose a description on the fly from `dimensions` by parsing the GENDER_<g>_AGE_<a>_RACE_<r> prefix (see _recipe). The hair/build/wardrobe/features fragments are then picked at RANDOM (a fresh look per job, identical across that job's panels), constrained by `age_restrictions` so a child never draws an adult-only fragment and vice versa. Enumerate the character explicitly under `characters` whenever you need a fixed look that stays byte-for-byte identical across every panel and every job.

_recipe.comment: How a character's `description` is composed from the modular tables below. characters[*].description is the COMPILED output and must be regenerated whenever any fragment it references changes -- it is the single source of truth at runtime, the refs are bookkeeping.
rules: gender_noun: use dimensions.gender[g].noun_child when dimensions.age[a].child is true, otherwise dimensions.gender[g].noun.
Articles are baked into dimensions.age[a].phrase (e.g. 'an 8-year-old', 'a 30-year-old', 'an elderly').
The trailing ', with {features}' segment is omitted entirely when the character has no `features` ref.
Keep descriptions appearance-only (age, ethnicity, build, hair, clothing, fixed features). Never bake in actions, expressions, poses or positions -- those belong in the per-panel story prompt.

hair.GREY_BUN: grey hair tied in a low bun
hair.PUFFS_BLACK: black hair in two puff buns

build.AVERAGE_MEDIUM: an average build and medium height
build.SLIM_TALL: a slim build and tall stature

wardrobe.CASUAL_TEE_JEANS: a plain navy crew-neck t-shirt and dark blue jeans
wardrobe.CARDIGAN_FLORAL: a soft beige cardigan over a floral blouse and a long grey skirt
wardrobe.BUSINESS_GREY_SUIT: a charcoal-grey business suit with a white shirt
wardrobe.SCHOOL_UNIFORM_BLUE: a navy school uniform with a white collared shirt
wardrobe.HOODIE_GREEN: a forest-green hoodie and black joggers
wardrobe.CASUAL_HOME_LILAC: a lilac knit jumper and comfortable beige trousers
wardrobe.CASUAL_HOME_BLUE: a light-blue checked shirt and grey chinos
wardrobe.DUNGAREES_RED: red dungarees over a white t-shirt
wardrobe.TSHIRT_STRIPED: a striped orange t-shirt and blue shorts
wardrobe.DRESS_PINK_FLORAL: a pink dress with a small flower print
wardrobe.TEE_TEAL_SHORTS: a teal t-shirt and grey shorts

age_restrictions._comment: Fragment keys reserved to one age group, consumed by workflow.py's RANDOM fallback composition (NOT enumerated descriptions). When a GENDER_..._AGE_..._RACE_... token has no `characters` entry, the random hair/build/wardrobe/features draw excludes keys reserved to the OTHER age group: a child (dimensions.age[a].child == true) skips `adult_only`, an adult skips `child_only`. A key in neither list suits both ages. If every option in a table is reserved away, the restriction is ignored for that table rather than producing nothing. Keep these in sync when you add a fragment whose look is clearly child- or adult-specific.

GENDER_F_AGE_70_RACE_ASIAN.description: an elderly East Asian woman with grey hair tied in a low bun, a petite build, wearing a soft beige cardigan over a floral blouse and a long grey skirt, with gentle smile lines

GENDER_M_AGE_25_RACE_ASIAN.description: a 25-year-old East Asian man with short, neatly combed black hair, a slim build and tall stature, wearing a forest-green hoodie and black joggers

GENDER_M_AGE_08_RACE_ASIAN.description: an 8-year-old East Asian boy with short, neatly combed black hair, a small child's frame, wearing a navy school uniform with a white collared shirt

GENDER_M_AGE_35_RACE_WHITE.description: a 35-year-old White man with short brown hair, an average build and medium height, wearing a charcoal-grey business suit with a white shirt, with light stubble

GENDER_F_AGE_30_RACE_BLACK.description: a 30-year-old Black woman with short curly black hair, a slim build and tall stature, wearing a light yellow summer dress, with a warm, kind smile

GENDER_F_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT.description: a 35-year-old East Asian woman with long straight black hair, an average build and medium height, wearing a lilac knit jumper and comfortable beige trousers, with a warm, kind smile

GENDER_M_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT.description: a 35-year-old East Asian man with short, neatly combed black hair, an average build and medium height, wearing a light-blue checked shirt and grey chinos, with a warm, kind smile

GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_BLACK.description: a 6-year-old Black girl with black hair in two puff buns, a small child's frame, wearing red dungarees over a white t-shirt, with a warm, kind smile

GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_WHITE.description: a 6-year-old White boy with short brown hair, a small child's frame, wearing a striped orange t-shirt and blue shorts, with light freckles across the cheeks

GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN.description: a 6-year-old East Asian girl with black hair in two pigtails, a small child's frame, wearing a pink dress with a small flower print, with a warm, kind smile

GENDER_M_AGE_06_RACE_SOUTH_ASIAN.description: a 6-year-old South Asian boy with short curly black hair, a small child's frame, wearing a teal t-shirt and grey shorts, with a warm, kind smile
